review_your_mission_objectives_and_cre: Review your mission objectives and crew assignment
countdown_and_liftoff_from_kennedy_spa: Countdown and liftoff from Kennedy Space Center
reach_low_earth_orbit_and_prepare_for_: Reach low Earth orbit and prepare for trans-lunar injection
pilot_the_lunar_module_to_the_surface: Pilot the Lunar Module to the surface
explore_the_lunar_surface_and_collect_: Explore the lunar surface and collect samples
launch_from_the_moon_and_rendezvous_wi: Launch from the Moon and rendezvous with Command Module
survive_re_entry_and_splash_down_in_th: Survive re-entry and splash down in the Pacific
pilots_the_lunar_module_to_the_surface: Pilots the Lunar Module to the surface and leads the EVA
orbits_the_moon_alone_in_the_command_m: Orbits the Moon alone in the Command Module while CDR and LMP explore
assists_with_descent_and_eva_operates_: Assists with descent and EVA, operates scientific instruments
this_ancient_rock_from_the_lunar_highl: This ancient rock from the lunar highlands is 4.4 billion years old — nearly as old as the Moon itself. It tells us the Moon once had a global magma ocean.
dark_volcanic_basalt_filled_the_moon_s: Dark volcanic basalt filled the Moon's giant impact basins to create the dark "seas" (maria) visible from Earth. These lavas erupted 3-3.5 billion years ago.
a_jumbled_mix_of_rock_fragments_welded: A jumbled mix of rock fragments welded together by meteorite impacts. The Moon's surface has been pounded for 4+ billion years.
lunar_soil_is_ground_up_rock_from_bill: Lunar soil is ground-up rock from billions of years of micrometeorite bombardment. It contains tiny glass beads and even traces of solar wind particles.
apollo_17_found_orange_soil_tiny_glass: Apollo 17 found orange soil — tiny glass beads from an ancient volcanic eruption 3.7 billion years ago. This was one of Apollo's most exciting discoveries!
kreep_stands_for_potassium_k_rare_eart: KREEP stands for Potassium (K), Rare Earth Elements, and Phosphorus. These minerals concentrated in the last dregs of the lunar magma ocean.
meteorite_impacts_melt_rock_into_glass: Meteorite impacts melt rock into glass that flies through space and lands as tiny spheres. Some contain trapped gases from the ancient lunar atmosphere.
apollo_15_found_this_4_1_billion_year_: Apollo 15 found this 4.1 billion year old anorthosite, one of the oldest rocks ever collected. It helped prove the magma ocean theory of the Moon's formation.
the_moon_is_about_384_400_km_away_ligh: The Moon is about 384,400 km away — light takes 1.3 seconds to travel there!
apollo_missions_took_about_3_days_each: Apollo missions took about 3 days each way, traveling at ~3,900 km/h.
the_moon_s_gravity_is_1_6_of_earth_s_a: The Moon's gravity is 1/6 of Earth's. A 70 kg person weighs only ~12 kg there!
the_sunlit_side_reaches_127_c_while_th: The sunlit side reaches 127°C, while the dark side drops to -173°C!
12_astronauts_walked_on_the_moon_acros: 12 astronauts walked on the Moon across Apollo 11, 12, 14, 15, 16, and 17.
buzz_aldrin_said_contact_light_first_b: Buzz Aldrin said "Contact light" first, but Armstrong's famous line started with "That's."
the_first_stage_burned_rp_1_kerosene_w: The first stage burned RP-1 kerosene with liquid oxygen — 2,000+ tons of fuel in 2.5 minutes!
sound_needs_a_medium_air_water_to_trav: Sound needs a medium (air/water) to travel. The Moon has no atmosphere, so it's perfectly silent.
every_apollo_astronaut_reported_that_m: Every Apollo astronaut reported that Moon dust smells like spent gunpowder when brought inside the LM!
the_oldest_moon_rocks_are_4_4_billion_: The oldest Moon rocks are 4.4 billion years old — nearly as old as the solar system itself!
the_moon_is_about_384_400_km_away_ligh_2: The Moon is about 384,400 km away — light takes 1.3 seconds to travel there!
rotate_spacecraft_to_expose_vent_to_su: Rotate spacecraft to expose vent to sunlight
reroute_cabin_heater_duct_to_warm_the_: Reroute cabin heater duct to warm the pipe
seal_the_vent_and_use_backup_waste_bag: Seal the vent and use backup waste bags
abort_the_descent_fire_ascent_engine: Abort the descent — fire ascent engine
take_manual_control_and_fly_past_the_b: Take manual control and fly past the boulders
abort_and_try_again_next_orbit: Abort and try again next orbit
immediately_isolate_tank_2_and_switch_: Immediately isolate Tank 2 and switch to Tank 1
try_to_reset_the_tank_heater_circuit: Try to reset the tank heater circuit
administer_anti_nausea_medication_and_: Administer anti-nausea medication and rest period
tough_it_out_keep_working_through_the_: Tough it out — keep working through the nausea
reduce_visual_stimulation_and_close_wi: Reduce visual stimulation and close window shades
realistic_tight_fuel_budget_faster_o_d: Realistic — tight fuel budget, faster O₂ drain
full_mission_simulation_launch_to_spla: Full mission simulation • Launch to splashdown
animated_saturn_v_rocket_launch_sequen: Animated Saturn V rocket launch sequence. 5-second countdown followed by ascent through atmosphere to orbit. Shows altitude, velocity, G-force, and stage separations.
saturn_v_3_stages_7_5_million_lbs_thru: 🚀 Saturn V • 3 stages • 7.5 million lbs thrust
watch_the_countdown_and_ascent_through: Watch the countdown and ascent through Earth's atmosphere
proceed_to_earth_orbit_phase_after_suc: Proceed to Earth orbit phase after successful launch
animated_view_of_spacecraft_in_low_ear: Animated view of spacecraft in low Earth orbit at 185 kilometers. CSM completes 1.5 orbits while a trans-lunar injection burn window aligns with the Moon's future position. Orbit counter, altitude, velocity, and TLI readiness displayed.
altitude_185_km_speed_28_000_km_h_1_5_: Altitude: 185 km • Speed: 28,000 km/h • 1.5 orbits before TLI burn
the_s_ivb_third_stage_will_fire_for_5_: The S-IVB third stage will fire for 5 minutes 47 seconds to accelerate from 28,000 km/h to 38,900 km/h — escape velocity. This single burn sends you on a trajectory to the Moon, 384,400 km away.
execute_trans_lunar_injection_burn_to_: Execute trans-lunar injection burn to begin 3-day journey to the Moon
execute_tli_burn_head_to_the_moon: 🚀 Execute TLI Burn — Head to the Moon
animated_trans_lunar_coast_earth_shrin: Animated trans-lunar coast. Earth shrinks on the left, Moon grows on the right as the spacecraft travels 384,400 kilometers over 3 days. Shows distance counter and mission communications.
arrive_at_the_moon_and_enter_lunar_orb: Arrive at the Moon and enter lunar orbit at 110 kilometer altitude
arrive_at_the_moon_enter_lunar_orbit: 🌕 Arrive at the Moon — Enter Lunar Orbit
animated_view_of_spacecraft_orbiting_t: Animated view of spacecraft orbiting the Moon at 110 kilometer altitude. Shows the Moon surface with craters, Sea of Tranquility landing site marked in green, and the spacecraft dot orbiting.
undock_lunar_module_eagle_from_command: Undock Lunar Module Eagle from Command Module Columbia and begin powered descent to the Moon surface
start_slowing_down_early_moon_gravity_: Start slowing down early — Moon gravity is gentle but relentless
watch_your_fuel_gauge_you_can_t_thrust: Watch your fuel gauge — you can't thrust without fuel!
reduce_horizontal_speed_before_focusin: Reduce horizontal speed before focusing on vertical
the_real_apollo_11_landed_with_only_25: The real Apollo 11 landed with only 25 seconds of fuel left!
interactive_lunar_descent_piloting_gam: Interactive lunar descent piloting game. Use W or Up Arrow for thrust, A and D or Left and Right arrows for lateral movement. Land with vertical speed under 3 meters per second and horizontal speed under 5 meters per second.
begin_extravehicular_activity_moonwalk: Begin extravehicular activity moonwalk to explore the lunar surface and collect geological samples
collect_at_least_4_unique_rock_samples: Collect at least 4 unique rock samples to satisfy the geology quest hook.
apollo_11_brought_back_47_5_lb_of_luna: Apollo 11 brought back 47.5 lb of lunar samples; Apollo 17 brought 243 lb.
in_one_sixth_gravity_a_hop_covers_abou: In one-sixth gravity, a hop covers about six times the horizontal distance for the same effort.
earth_hangs_in_a_fixed_spot_in_the_lun: Earth hangs in a fixed spot in the lunar sky because the Moon is tidally locked.
begin_eva_on_the_lunar_surface: Begin EVA on the lunar surface
step_onto_the_moon_begin_eva: 👨‍🚀 Step Onto the Moon · Begin EVA
webgl_failed_to_initialize_your_browse: WebGL failed to initialize. Your browser or device might not support 3D hardware acceleration.
interactive_3d_lunar_surface_eva_use_w: Interactive 3D lunar surface EVA. Use WASD to walk, Space to jump in one-sixth gravity, F to collect rock samples, mouse to look around. Collect geological samples and explore the Moon surface near the Lunar Module.
the_apollo_lunar_surface_experiments_p: The Apollo Lunar Surface Experiments Package ran for years after the astronauts left. The seismometer detected moonquakes and meteorite impacts until 1977.
the_lunar_roving_vehicle_cost_38_milli: The Lunar Roving Vehicle cost $38 million. Apollo 17's rover traveled 35.7 km — still parked on the Moon with the keys in it!
the_flags_on_the_moon_have_been_bleach: The flags on the Moon have been bleached pure white by decades of unfiltered UV radiation. Only the Apollo 12 flag was knocked over by engine exhaust.
lunar_seismometers_detected_deep_moonq: Lunar seismometers detected deep moonquakes at 700-1100 km depth, caused by tidal forces from Earth. The Moon still has a partially molten core!
scientists_bounce_lasers_off_this_mirr: Scientists bounce lasers off this mirror to measure the Earth-Moon distance to within 1 cm accuracy. The Moon moves 3.8 cm farther from Earth each year.
explore_collect_samples_jump_in_1_6_gr: Explore • Collect samples • Jump in 1/6 gravity!
animated_lunar_ascent_sequence_ascent_: Animated lunar ascent sequence. Ascent stage lifts off the Moon leaving the descent stage behind, climbs to lunar orbit over 60 kilometers, then rendezvous-docks with the orbiting Command Module Columbia. HUD shows altitude, phase, distance to CSM.
ascent_stage_launches_from_moon_docks_: Ascent stage launches from Moon, docks with Columbia
the_lm_s_ascent_engine_a_single_start_: The LM's ascent engine — a single-start hypergolic motor with no abort option — fires to launch you off the lunar surface. The descent stage serves as the launch pad and stays behind. You rendezvous and dock with Columbia, then jettison "Eagle" (it eventually crashes into the Moon).
fire_trans_earth_injection_burn_to_beg: Fire trans-Earth injection burn to begin 3-day return journey home
the_service_module_engine_fires_for_th: The Service Module engine fires for the Trans-Earth Injection burn. You coast for 3 days back to Earth, jettison the Service Module, and prepare the Command Module for re-entry — the most dangerous phase of the mission.
begin_atmospheric_re_entry_sequence_at: Begin atmospheric re-entry sequence at 39,900 kilometers per hour
animated_re_entry_sequence_command_mod: Animated re-entry sequence. Command Module enters atmosphere at 39,900 km/h with plasma heating to 2,760 degrees. Shows radio blackout, drogue chutes, main parachutes, and ocean splashdown.
watch_the_command_module_survive_re_en: Watch the Command Module survive re-entry at 39,900 km/h through 2,760°C plasma, deploy parachutes, and splash down in the Pacific Ocean.
complete_the_mission_with_pacific_ocea: Complete the mission with Pacific Ocean splashdown. Welcome home Commander!
welcome_home_commander_the_world_celeb: Welcome home, Commander. The world celebrates.
these_samples_will_be_studied_by_scien: These samples will be studied by scientists for decades to come.
reset_and_start_a_new_moon_mission_fro: Reset and start a new Moon mission from the beginning
tsiolkovsky_rocket_equation_adjust_mas: Tsiolkovsky rocket equation. Adjust mass ratio, burn duration, and specific impulse (Isp). Discrete 3-state outcome shows whether your delta-V is insufficient, achieves LEO, or escapes Earth. No score, no reveal.
hypothesis_free_text_no_right_answer_w: Hypothesis (free text — no right answer): Which lever matters most?
hold_two_sliders_steady_move_one_watch: Hold two sliders steady. Move one. Watch.
find_two_settings_producing_the_same_o: Find two settings producing the same outcome.
which_slider_affects_v_the_most_use_th: Which slider affects Δv the most? Use the log.
real_spacecraft_trade_fuel_mass_agains: Real spacecraft trade fuel mass against Isp. Investigate why.
i_think_i_understand_the_trade_offs: I think I understand the trade-offs
explain_in_your_own_words_how_do_mass_: Explain in your own words: how do mass ratio, burn duration, and Isp interact?
design_note_discrete_3_state_outcome_n: Design note: discrete 3-state outcome; no score; no reveal — by design.
